name: Monstera Deliciosa
description: A trendy houseplant with exotic, holey leaves, perfect for indoor decoration.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top of the medium (2 cm) is dry, roughly every 2-3 days.
careDetails.pruning: Cut off yellowing or damaged leaves at the base of the stem.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply liquid foliar fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaves turn yellow if overwatered.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — optimal leaf growth visible within 2–3 months
hidroponik.tips: Replace the nutrient solution every 2 weeks. Make sure about ⅓ of the roots are submerged and leave an air gap above the solution so the roots can breathe.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — mouth/throat irritation, drooling if chewed

name: Aglaonema
description: Chinese Evergreen, a houseplant with beautiful reddish leaf patterns that is easy to care for.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days, making sure the pot has good drainage.
careDetails.sunlight: Shade or moderate light; avoid direct sun.
careDetails.pruning: Remove any flowers that appear so nutrients stay focused on the leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Slow-release Dekastar fertilizer every 3 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Root rot caused by dense, waterlogged growing medium.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — full growth visible within 3–4 months
hidroponik.tips: Use a dark container to prevent algae from growing in the nutrient solution. Aglaonema is very well suited to the semi-hydroponic method using hydroton.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, vomiting, drooling

name: Snake Plant
description: A tough, pollutant-absorbing plant with stiff, upright, sword-like leaves.

careDetails.watering: Very infrequently; once a week or every 2 weeks is enough.
careDetails.sunlight: Adaptable, from low light to full sun.
careDetails.pruning: Only cut off broken or wilted leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Rarely needs fertilizer; add compost twice a year.
careDetails.commonProblems: Almost none, except dying from being watered too often.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Very diluted AB Mix 400–600 ppm (EC 0.8–1.2) — this plant doesn't need much nutrition
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — steady growth visible within 2–4 months
hidroponik.tips: Don't submerge the entire root system — let the top ⅓ stay dry to prevent rot. Change the water every 3–4 weeks since this plant grows slowly.

toxicity.note: Saponins — nausea, vomiting, diarrhea in pets

name: Moon Orchid
description: Indonesia's charm flower, with large, elegant, long-lasting white blooms.

careDetails.watering: Mist the roots and coconut-husk medium every morning.
careDetails.pruning: Cut off all flower stalks once they have wilted.
careDetails.fertilizer: Spray on a flowering fertilizer once a week.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf rot caused by water trapped between the leaves.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (LECA) — the most recommended medium for orchids
hidroponik.nutrisi: Special liquid orchid fertilizer 300–500 ppm (EC 0.6–1.0) — a very low dose
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — blooms optimally after 6–12 months of acclimation
hidroponik.tips: Let the LECA wick water up by capillary action; don't fully submerge the roots. Use a transparent pot so you can monitor the condition of the healthy green roots.

toxicity.note: Phalaenopsis orchids are non-toxic to cats and dogs

name: Weeping Fig Bonsai
description: A classic bonsai with beautiful aerial roots, very popular in Indonesia.

careDetails.watering: Water every day during the dry season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune regularly to shape the canopy.
careDetails.fertilizer: Slow-release NPK fertilizer every 2 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs on the leaves and root rot if the pot has no drainage holes.

hidroponik.tips: Weeping fig bonsai needs a special soil medium to develop nebari (surface roots) and is not suited to hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Ficus sap irritates mouth, skin; causes vomiting

name: Philodendron
description: A tropical climbing plant with glossy green leaves, easy to care for and beautifully trailing.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2 cm of medium is dry; it must never be waterlogged.
careDetails.pruning: Trim the tips of the vines to encourage branching.
careDetails.fertilizer: Liquid NPK fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaves turn yellow from overwatering or insufficient light.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — active vine growth visible within 1–2 months
hidroponik.tips: Philodendron adapts very easily to water. Just place stem cuttings in clean water for 2 weeks to root them before moving to the nutrient solution.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, drooling, vomiting

name: Golden Pothos
description: Pothos, a super hardy climbing plant great for beginners that can live in water or soil.

careDetails.watering: Water 1-2 times a week; let the medium dry out a bit first.
careDetails.sunlight: Very tolerant of low light, but grows best in indirect light.
careDetails.pruning: Cut back overly long vines to maintain its shape.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted liquid fertilizer every 2 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Yellow leaves from being watered too often.

hidroponik.media: No medium — roots directly in water
hidroponik.nutrisi: Very diluted AB Mix 400–700 ppm (EC 0.8–1.4) or even plain water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — thrives within 2–4 weeks
hidroponik.tips: Golden pothos is the best plant for hydroponic beginners — just put a cutting in a clear water bottle and place it somewhere bright. Change the water every 1–2 weeks.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — mouth irritation, drooling, vomiting

name: ZZ Plant
description: A glossy plant that is extremely drought-tolerant, perfect for an office or bedroom.

careDetails.watering: Water very infrequently, once every 1-2 weeks; let it dry out completely.
careDetails.pruning: Cut yellowing leaves off at the base of the stalk.
careDetails.fertilizer: Slow-release fertilizer every 3 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Rhizome rot if watered too often.

hidroponik.tips: The ZZ Plant has water-storing rhizomes that are very prone to rot if submerged in solution — not recommended for hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, vomiting if chewed

name: Cactus
description: A spiny cactus with bright seasonal flowers; it needs little water and minimal care.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 weeks, less often during the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: No pruning needed; just clean off the dried parts.
careDetails.fertilizer: Special cactus fertilizer twice a year.
careDetails.commonProblems: Base rot if water pools in the medium.

hidroponik.tips: Cacti are adapted to extremely dry conditions — a constantly wet hydroponic system completely contradicts their basic needs.

toxicity.note: Not chemically toxic, but spines cause physical injury

name: Calathea
description: An exotic plant with white-striped leaves that are purple underneath; its leaves move uniquely to follow the light.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days with settled, chlorine-free water.
careDetails.sunlight: Shaded indirect light, away from direct AC airflow.
careDetails.pruning: Cut dried leaves off at the base.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted liquid NPK fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown leaf tips from chlorine in the water or air that is too dry.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton or a perlite + cocopeat mix
hidroponik.nutrisi: Diluted AB Mix 600–800 ppm (EC 1.2–1.6) — use chlorine/fluoride-free water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — the leaf pattern grows more beautiful after 2–3 months of acclimation
hidroponik.tips: Calathea is very sensitive to minerals in water — use rainwater or water that has settled for 24 hours. Avoid placing it near direct AC airflow.

toxicity.note: Calathea is non-toxic and safe for cats, dogs

name: Spinach
description: A leafy green that is very easy to grow in the home garden, rich in iron.

careDetails.watering: Water twice a day, morning and evening.
careDetails.pruning: No pruning needed; harvested by pulling the whole plant.
careDetails.fertilizer: Urea or manure fertilizer 2 weeks after planting.
careDetails.commonProblems: Eaten by leaf caterpillars and grasshoppers.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 14–20 days after transplanting (very fast!)
hidroponik.tips: Sow the seeds in rockwool first for 5–7 days, then move them to the NFT system. It's a great fit for a simple household-scale floating raft (DWC) system.

toxicity.note: Amaranthus may cause kidney issues in large amounts

name: Water Spinach
description: A favorite vegetable of Indonesians, fast-growing with a short harvest time (3-4 weeks).

careDetails.watering: Needs a lot of water; keep the medium consistently moist.
careDetails.pruning: You can cut off the top when harvesting so new shoots grow.
careDetails.fertilizer: Liquid NPK fertilizer every 10 days.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf spots from fungus if planted too densely.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Vegetable AB Mix 1000–1500 ppm (EC 2.0–3.0) — water spinach needs high nutrition
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 21–30 days from sowing — can be multi-harvested by cutting the top
hidroponik.tips: Water spinach grows very aggressively in hydroponics — it can grow twice as fast as in soil. First harvest at 21 days, then cut and let it grow again.

toxicity.note: Water spinach generally safe for cats and dogs

name: Cherry Tomato
description: Small, sweet tomatoes, perfect for growing in hanging pots or polybags on the patio.

careDetails.watering: Water regularly every morning.
careDetails.pruning: Remove the old lower leaves that touch the soil.
careDetails.fertilizer: High-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer once it starts flowering.
careDetails.commonProblems: Blossom-end rot from a lack of calcium.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1200–2500 ppm (EC 2.5–5.0) — increase during fruiting
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 55–75 days from sowing — 2 weeks faster than soil-grown
hidroponik.tips: Tie the tomato stem to a vertical stake since it grows very fast in hydroponics. When it flowers, gently shake the stem daily to aid pollination.

toxicity.note: Ripe fruit safe; leaves, stems, unripe fruit toxic

name: Bird's Eye Chili
description: Indonesia's favorite hot chili, very productive in pots and can be harvested many times.

careDetails.watering: Water every day; don't let it wilt.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours a day.
careDetails.pruning: Remove the first flower so the plant grows stronger.
careDetails.fertilizer: KCl and TSP fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fusarium wilt and red spider mite pests.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1000–2000 ppm (EC 2.0–4.0) — increase potassium during fruiting
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 75–90 days from sowing — productivity can be 3 times higher in hydroponics
hidroponik.tips: Bird's eye chili is the best candidate for long-term hydroponics — a single plant can keep producing for 1–2 years. Make sure the solution is well aerated with an air pump.

toxicity.note: Capsaicin irritates mouth, stomach; causes vomiting

name: Lettuce
description: Fresh salad greens that can be harvested in just 30-40 days, ideal for a balcony.

careDetails.watering: Water regularly every day; keep the medium moist.
careDetails.sunlight: Morning sun; avoid the harsh midday heat as it wilts quickly.
careDetails.pruning: Pick only the outer leaves so it keeps growing.
careDetails.fertilizer: Liquid nitrogen fertilizer every 2 weeks.
careDetails.commonProblems: Bolts quickly (premature flowering) in temperatures that are too hot.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 25–35 days after transplanting — this is the most popular plant for hydroponic beginners!
hidroponik.tips: Lettuce is the best plant for learning hydroponics. With the Kratky method (a used bottle), you don't need a pump at all — just fill it with nutrients and wait to harvest.

toxicity.note: Lettuce is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Mustard Greens
description: A broad-leafed vegetable for stir-fries, with a quick harvest in 30-40 days from planting.

careDetails.watering: Water every morning; the medium must stay consistently moist.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun; grows better in the dry season.
careDetails.pruning: Not needed; harvest all at once by pulling the plant.
careDetails.fertilizer: Urea fertilizer two weeks after planting.
careDetails.commonProblems: Cabbage worms and aphids.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 25–35 days after transplanting
hidroponik.tips: A floating raft system is ideal for mustard greens — use a piece of scrap styrofoam as the raft with 5 cm holes. Great for household-scale production.

toxicity.note: Brassica safe in small amounts; excess causes gas, bloating

name: Cat's Whiskers
description: A medicinal plant with flowers resembling a cat's whiskers, known to help with urinary tract health.

careDetails.watering: Water once a day; it loves moist soil.
careDetails.pruning: Prune after heavy flowering to stimulate new shoots.
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer every month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphid pests on the tips of young shoots.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 45–60 days from sowing — harvest the leaves and young flowers
hidroponik.tips: The active compound orthosiphonin is higher in plants under mild stress — don't give too much nutrition. Harvest before heavy flowering for the highest levels.

toxicity.note: Limited pet data; diuretic herb — give with caution

name: Green Chiretta
description: The 'king of bitters,' with remarkable benefits for reducing fever and boosting immunity.

careDetails.watering: Drought-tolerant; water when the soil is dry.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun is better for its active-compound content.
careDetails.pruning: Cut off the top before flowering to harvest the leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Compost fertilizer at the start of planting.
careDetails.commonProblems: Rarely affected by pests because of its very bitter taste.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 700–1000 ppm (EC 1.4–2.0) — low so the andrographolide content is optimal
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–75 days — harvest the leaves before the plant flowers
hidroponik.tips: Green chiretta grows well in hydroponics, though it's rarely done. The bitter content (andrographolide) is actually higher in plants that are slightly 'deprived' — don't over-fertilize.

toxicity.note: Bitter medicinal herb; limited safety data for pets

name: Betel
description: A traditional Indonesian climbing plant with naturally antiseptic leaves for oral and skin health.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; it likes a moist but not waterlogged medium.
careDetails.sunlight: Shade; it doesn't like direct sun for too long.
careDetails.pruning: Trim overly long vines and old leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Manure fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fungus on the leaves if it's too humid with no air circulation.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton or roots directly in water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Harvest the leaves after 3–4 weeks of acclimation, then continuously
hidroponik.tips: Betel is very easy to propagate from cuttings and grow in water — a good fit as a hanging hydroponic plant indoors. Make sure there's a pole or netting for the vines to climb.

toxicity.note: Essential oils/phenols may irritate; limited pet data

name: Gotu Kola
description: A popular herb for improving brain function and skin-cell regeneration, growing as a creeper.

careDetails.watering: Likes moisture; water every 2 days or when the medium starts to dry out.
careDetails.sunlight: Shade; grows well under trees or shade netting.
careDetails.pruning: Trim the runners that creep out of the pot.
careDetails.fertilizer: Liquid organic fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Dies if deprived of water for a long time.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 30–45 days — harvest the young leaves regularly
hidroponik.tips: Gotu kola spreads quickly in an NFT system. The asiaticoside (main active compound) content is higher in plants harvested young — harvest every 2 weeks.

toxicity.note: Gotu kola generally non-toxic to cats and dogs

name: Noni
description: A shrub with unusual fruit, valued as an immune stimulant and a powerful antioxidant.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; drought-tolerant once mature.
careDetails.pruning: Prune the branches to control size and encourage fruiting.
careDetails.fertilizer: Manure and compost every 2 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit rots if overwatered during the rainy season.

hidroponik.tips: Noni is a shrub that grows large (2–6 meters). Its root system and size make it unsuitable for home hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Noni fruit edible; limited pet toxicity data

name: Red Ginger
description: A spicy rhizome that warms the body, popular for hot herbal drinks and jamu.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; avoid standing water so the rhizome doesn't rot.
careDetails.sunlight: A somewhat shaded spot; avoid harsh direct sunlight.
careDetails.pruning: Remove yellowing leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Manure and kitchen ash every month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Bacterial wilt disease and rhizome rot.

hidroponik.tips: Red ginger is a rhizome plant that needs soil space to develop its rhizomes. A hydroponic system is not suitable for optimal ginger rhizome growth.

toxicity.note: Ginger is non-toxic; large amounts may upset stomach

name: Aloe Vera
description: A versatile medicinal plant for skin, hair, and digestive health.

careDetails.watering: Water every 5-7 days; make sure the soil is completely dry before watering again.
careDetails.sunlight: Abundant bright light; morning sun is very good.
careDetails.pruning: Cut the lowest leaves with a sharp knife when harvesting.
careDetails.fertilizer: Slow-release NPK fertilizer every 3 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf-base rot from overwatering.

hidroponik.tips: Aloe vera is a succulent that stores water in its leaves — too much water will actually make the plant rot. A hydroponic system is not recommended.

toxicity.note: Aloe latex/saponins — vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy

name: Turmeric
description: A golden-yellow rhizome rich in curcumin for anti-inflammatory benefits, a kitchen-spice staple in every home.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; the medium must be moist but not waterlogged.
careDetails.sunlight: Shade to partial shade; avoid harsh direct sun.
careDetails.pruning: Let it grow naturally; cut off the parts that dry out.
careDetails.fertilizer: Manure and compost every month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Rhizome rot from poor drainage.

hidroponik.tips: Turmeric is a rhizome plant that needs deep, loose soil to develop large yellow rhizomes. Not suitable for hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Turmeric is non-toxic; safe in small amounts

name: Lemongrass
description: A versatile, lemon-scented clumping plant that repels mosquitoes while serving as a cooking herb.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; tolerates short dry spells.
careDetails.pruning: Trim away the dry, browned outer leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply manure fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Rarely bothered by pests thanks to its strong scent.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton or roots directly in water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–90 days — harvest the thickened lower stalks
hidroponik.tips: Place fresh lemongrass stalks (leftovers from cooking) in plain water for 2–3 weeks until they root, then move them into the nutrient solution. This is the easiest way to start lemongrass hydroponically.

toxicity.note: Essential oils may cause mild stomach upset in pets

name: Aromatic Ginger (Kencur)
description: A small rhizome with big potential for throat health and relieving coughs.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; reduce during the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: Let it wilt naturally in the dry season—the rhizome stays alive in the soil.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply organic fertilizer every 2 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: The rhizome fails to develop if the medium is too compact.

hidroponik.tips: Aromatic ginger is a shallow-rhizome plant that needs a loose growing medium. Its rhizomes cannot develop properly in a water-based hydroponic system.

toxicity.note: Edible rhizome; limited pet toxicity data — use caution

name: Rosemary
description: A distinctively fragrant plant for Western cooking and a calming essential oil.

careDetails.watering: Very prone to overwatering. Water only when the growing medium is dry all the way to the bottom.
careDetails.pruning: Trim the tips of the branches to maintain shape and for harvesting.
careDetails.fertilizer: Avoid over-fertilizing; apply a thin layer of compost every 2 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Often dies suddenly from root rot caused by an insufficiently porous medium.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Herbal AB Mix 700–1000 ppm (EC 1.4–2.0) — keep it from being too high so the aroma stays strong
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–90 days — routinely harvest 5–10 cm branch tips
hidroponik.tips: Rosemary in hydroponics must have very good solution aeration — use a powerful air pump. Essential oil content is higher when the plant is slightly 'stressed' — reduce nutrients by 20% from the standard dose.

toxicity.note: Rosemary is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Lavender
description: A purple flower with a calming aroma that effectively repels mosquitoes and aids sleep.

careDetails.watering: Water sparingly; let it dry out between waterings.
careDetails.pruning: Trim flowers as they begin to fade to keep it blooming.
careDetails.fertilizer: A little dolomite lime and compost.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fungus in tropical climates that are too humid and lack airflow.

hidroponik.tips: Lavender is very prone to root rot in humid tropical climates — a hydroponic system makes this worse. It is better grown in a perlite + sand medium with extreme drainage.

toxicity.note: Linalool/linalyl acetate — vomiting, lethargy if ingested

name: Mint
description: A fresh, fast-growing mint plant, perfect for refreshing drinks, salads, and aromatherapy.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; it likes moist soil.
careDetails.sunlight: Moderate indirect light; it can grow well indoors.
careDetails.pruning: Prune aggressively to prevent flowering and preserve the aroma.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a liquid nitrogen fertilizer every month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Spreads quickly (invasive); it is best planted in a separate pot.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 25–40 days from cuttings — one of the best herbs for hydroponics!
hidroponik.tips: Mint grows VERY fast in hydroponics — it can be harvested within a month. Take a fresh mint stem cutting from the market, put it in water, wait 1–2 weeks for it to root, then move it into the nutrient solution.

toxicity.note: Essential oils — vomiting, diarrhea in cats, dogs

name: Pandan
description: An aromatic plant characteristic of Southeast Asia, used as a natural food fragrance and for aromatherapy.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; it likes a fairly moist medium.
careDetails.sunlight: Shade to partial shade; avoid direct, overly harsh sun.
careDetails.pruning: Trim old or torn leaves to keep it looking neat.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply manure fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf tips dry out if it gets too dry or lacks nutrients.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton or roots directly in water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Harvest leaves after 2–3 months of acclimation — choose the older leaves
hidroponik.tips: Pandan is very easy to grow hydroponically — take an offshoot that already has roots and place it in a nutrient-filled bucket. Pandan's aroma is actually stronger in plants under slight water stress.

toxicity.note: Pandan is non-toxic and safe for cats, dogs

name: Eggplant
description: A glossy purple fruiting vegetable rich in antioxidants, well suited to growing in large polybags.

careDetails.watering: Water every morning; never let the medium dry out.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6-8 hours a day.
careDetails.pruning: Remove excess side shoots so the fruit grows larger.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a high-K NPK fertilizer during the flowering and fruiting stage.
careDetails.commonProblems: Stem borers and whiteflies.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–80 days from sowing — more uniform fruit and a larger size
hidroponik.tips: Eggplant needs a stable system with good aeration. Use net pots at least 10 cm in size and make sure the air pump runs 24 hours so the roots don't run short of oxygen.

toxicity.note: Ripe fruit ok; leaves, stems contain toxic solanine

name: Cucumber
description: A fresh, healthy fruiting vegetable that can climb a fence or a balcony trellis.

careDetails.watering: Water every day; this plant needs a lot of water.
careDetails.pruning: Remove old leaves and unproductive branches.
careDetails.fertilizer: Alternate manure and NPK fertilizer every 10 days.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew on the leaves in humid conditions without airflow.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 35–50 days from sowing — far faster and more productive than growing in soil
hidroponik.tips: Cucumber climbs very fast in hydroponics — prepare a stake or a vertical climbing net. Harvest as often as possible (every 2 days) to keep the plant producing.

toxicity.note: Cucumber is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Carrot
description: An orange root vegetable rich in beta-carotene; it needs a loose, deep soil medium to grow well.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; keep moisture even.
careDetails.pruning: Thin the foliage so light can reach all the plants.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply potassium and phosphate fertilizer to encourage root development.
careDetails.commonProblems: The roots fork if the soil is too compact or contains stones.

hidroponik.tips: Carrot is a root crop that needs deep, loose soil space to grow. A standard hydroponic system does not support optimal root development.

toxicity.note: Carrot is non-toxic and safe for cats, dogs

name: Green Beans
description: Nutritious green pods that fruit quickly (45-55 days); they can climb or grow upright depending on the variety.

careDetails.watering: Water every day, especially during the dry season.
careDetails.pruning: Harvest regularly to stimulate new pods.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a low-nitrogen NPK fertilizer once they begin to flower.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leaf rust and pod borers.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 45–55 days from sowing — slightly slower than leafy vegetables
hidroponik.tips: Choose an upright (bush bean) variety for hydroponics so it's easier to manage. Green beans fix their own nitrogen, so the N level in the solution can be slightly lower.

toxicity.note: Raw beans contain lectins; cook before feeding pets

name: Strawberry
description: A tiny red fruit with a sweet-tart flavor that grows beautifully in a hanging pot or on a patio.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2 days; keep water off the fruit and leaves to prevent rot.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun 6-8 hours; ideal temperature below 28°C.
careDetails.pruning: Remove excess runners so energy goes to the fruit.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a high-potassium (K) fertilizer for sweet, abundant fruit.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit rot from high humidity and poor air circulation.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1000–1500 ppm vegetative, 1500–2000 ppm (EC 3.0–4.0) when fruiting
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–90 days — fruit is redder, sweeter, and more uniform than soil-grown
hidroponik.tips: Strawberry is the most popular fruit for home hydroponics. An NFT system tilted 30–45° works very well because the fruit hangs down and is easy to harvest without getting dirty.

toxicity.note: Strawberry is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Papaya
description: A super-productive tropical fruit that can be harvested in 6-9 months and is very popular in Indonesian home gardens.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; it tolerates short dry spells but must not become waterlogged.
careDetails.pruning: It needs little pruning—just remove the old lower leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply manure + NPK fertilizer once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Papaya butterflies and stem base rot in wet soil.

hidroponik.tips: Papaya is a large tree that needs strong roots anchored deep in the soil. Its size and structural needs make a home hydroponic system unfeasible.

toxicity.note: Papaya flesh safe; remove seeds before feeding

name: Red Guava
description: A tropical fruit rich in vitamin C with sweet pink flesh; it can be grown in a large pot.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; reduce during the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune after harvest to stimulate new flowers.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply NPK fertilizer and compost once a month.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit rot caused by fruit flies; bag the young fruit with plastic covers.

hidroponik.tips: Guava is a tree with a large root system that needs deep soil. It is not suitable for a standard home hydroponic system.

toxicity.note: Guava is non-toxic; feed in moderation

name: Cavendish Banana
description: A popular banana variety with sweet, nutritious fruit that grows fast and produces well in tropical climates.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; it needs a lot of water in hot weather.
careDetails.pruning: Keep the single best sucker and remove the others so nutrients stay focused.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a high-potassium fertilizer for optimal fruit.
careDetails.commonProblems: Panama wilt disease (Fusarium) and root nematodes.

hidroponik.tips: Banana needs a very deep, strong root system in the soil. The plant's size and structural needs make a conventional hydroponic system unfeasible.

toxicity.note: Banana is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Key Lime
description: A versatile small lime for cooking, drinks, and health, well suited to growing in a medium-sized pot.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; let the top layer of soil dry out first.
careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, at least 8 hours a day.
careDetails.pruning: Prune crossing branches and dead branches.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a citrus-specific fertilizer or NPK every 3 months.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, scale insects, and greening disease.

hidroponik.tips: Key lime is a woody tree that grows 2–5 meters — its root system and size far exceed the capacity of a home hydroponic system.

toxicity.note: Citrus oils, psoralens — vomiting, diarrhea, irritation

name: Mini Watermelon
description: A small-sized watermelon that can be grown in a large polybag; the fruit is sweet with a thin rind.

careDetails.watering: Water every day; it needs a lot of water, especially during fruit formation.
careDetails.pruning: Keep 1-2 fruits per plant and remove excess female flowers.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a high-phosphorus fertilizer when flowering and potassium when fruiting.
careDetails.commonProblems: Blossom-end rot and thrips infestations.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1200–1800 ppm vegetative, up to 2500–3500 ppm (EC 5.0–7.0) when fruiting
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 65–80 days — more consistent fruit size and higher sugar content
hidroponik.tips: Support the watermelon fruit with a knitted net so it doesn't fall under its own weight. Reduce nitrogen and increase potassium once the fruit reaches about fist size.

toxicity.note: Watermelon flesh safe; avoid seeds and rind

name: Grape
description: An exotic fruit that climbs beautifully on a trellis; with a little patience it can fruit abundantly in Indonesia.

careDetails.watering: Water every 2-3 days; reduce drastically after major pruning.
careDetails.pruning: Do major pruning once a year to stimulate new fruiting shoots.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply potassium and boron fertilizer for abundant, sweet fruit.
careDetails.commonProblems: Powdery mildew and grape mites.

hidroponik.nutrisi: Fruiting AB Mix 1000–1500 ppm vegetative, 1500–2500 ppm (EC 3.0–5.0) when fruiting
hidroponik.waktu_panen: First year: vegetative. First harvest in year 2 — 90–120 days after flowering
hidroponik.tips: Hydroponic grapes need a sturdy installation because the vine is heavy as it climbs. Nutrient management must be precise — excess nitrogen produces lots of leaves but little fruit.

toxicity.note: Grapes cause kidney failure in dogs — never feed

name: Peace Lily
description: A popular indoor ornamental plant with elegant white flowers and air-filtering ability. Tolerant of low-light conditions, it suits a corner of a room.

careDetails.soil: Well-draining potting medium, rich in organic matter
careDetails.watering: Water when the medium starts to dry out
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced liquid fertilizer (NPK) every 4 weeks during the active growing season; reduce in cold weather or the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically trim old, dry, or damaged leaves or stems to stimulate new shoots and keep the plant looking neat.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs, spider mites, and root rot from an overly wet medium.

hidroponik.media: Rockwool or a pot with roots in water
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Ornamental plant, enjoyed year-round
hidroponik.tips: Peace lily is very easy in hydroponics — just place it in a pot of water with the roots partially submerged. Change the water every 1–2 weeks.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — oral irritation, drooling, vomiting

name: Anthurium
description: A tropical ornamental plant with striking red flowers and glossy leaves. It works as a premium indoor plant that lends a luxurious feel.

careDetails.soil: Orchid mix + perlite + cocopeat
careDetails.watering: Water regularly; keep the medium moist
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced liquid fertilizer (NPK) every 4 weeks during the active growing season; reduce in cold weather or the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically trim old, dry, or damaged leaves or stems to stimulate new shoots and keep the plant looking neat.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs, spider mites, and root rot from an overly wet medium.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Ornamental plant; flowers appear regularly every 3–4 months
hidroponik.tips: Anthurium in LECA is very popular — wash the LECA clean and submerge the roots partially in a diluted nutrient solution. Make sure there is air space for the roots to breathe.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — mouth pain, drooling, vomiting

name: Marble Queen Pothos
description: A pothos variety with beautiful white-and-green marbled leaves. Very hardy, tolerant of various conditions, and grows into a lovely trailing vine.

careDetails.soil: General potting medium with good drainage
careDetails.watering: Water when the top of the medium is dry
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced liquid fertilizer (NPK) every 4 weeks during the active growing season; reduce in cold weather or the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically trim old, dry, or damaged leaves or stems to stimulate new shoots and keep the plant looking neat.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs, spider mites, and root rot from an overly wet medium.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Trailing ornamental plant, enjoy it year-round
hidroponik.nutrisi: Liquid fertilizer at 1/4 dose, change water every 2 weeks
hidroponik.tips: Pothos is the easiest hydroponic plant — just put a cutting in a glass vase of water. Add a few pieces of activated charcoal to prevent odor and algae.

toxicity.note: Calcium oxalate — mouth irritation, drooling, vomiting

name: Rubber Plant
description: An upright-stemmed ornamental plant with large glossy leaves in dark green or reddish-brown. Bold and modern-looking for a corner of a room.

careDetails.soil: Potting medium with very good drainage
careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2–3 cm of the medium is dry
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced liquid fertilizer (NPK) every 4 weeks during the active growing season; reduce in cold weather or the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically trim old, dry, or damaged leaves or stems to stimulate new shoots and keep the plant looking neat.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs, spider mites, and root rot from an overly wet medium.

hidroponik.tips: The rubber plant is less than ideal for hydroponics because its root system prefers a medium that gets fairly dry between waterings. It is better grown in a pot with a well-draining medium.

toxicity.note: Ficus sap — mouth, skin irritation, vomiting

name: Bird of Paradise
description: A dramatic ornamental plant with large paddle-shaped leaves and orange-blue flowers resembling a bird. It brings a premium tropical feel to a room.

careDetails.soil: Fertile potting medium with good drainage
careDetails.watering: Water when the top of the medium dries out
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced liquid fertilizer (NPK) every 4 weeks during the active growing season; reduce in cold weather or the rainy season.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically trim old, dry, or damaged leaves or stems to stimulate new shoots and keep the plant looking neat.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs, spider mites, and root rot from an overly wet medium.

hidroponik.tips: Bird of Paradise is not recommended for hydroponics because its root system is large and prefers a fertile soil medium. It needs a large pot to grow well.

toxicity.note: Strelitzia — vomiting, drowsiness; seeds most toxic

name: Pak Choi
description: A fast-harvesting leafy green popular in Indonesia. Crisp texture and mild flavor, well suited to stir-fries and soups. A favorite for hydroponic beginners because it is so easy.

careDetails.soil: Fertile, well-draining medium, or hydroponics
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, 1–2x a day
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply a balanced NPK fertilizer or liquid compost every 2 weeks to support leaf and fruit growth.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically trim old, dry, or damaged leaves or stems to stimulate new shoots and keep the plant looking neat.
careDetails.commonProblems: Caterpillars, aphids, and leaf fungus when humidity is high.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 25–35 days — the first harvest can be done within 1 month
hidroponik.tips: Pak choi is the best hydroponic plant for beginners! It grows fast, harvests in 25–30 days, and tolerates nutrient variation. Start with the Kratky method in a used bottle.

toxicity.note: Brassica safe in small amounts; excess causes gas

name: Bell Pepper
description: A bell-shaped pepper in red, yellow, or green that's rich in vitamin C. Sweeter than regular chili peppers, popular in Western dishes and salads.

careDetails.soil: Fertile, compost-rich medium, pH 6.0–6.8
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, avoid waterlogging
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply balanced NPK fertilizer or liquid compost every 2 weeks to support leaf and fruit growth.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Caterpillars, aphids, and leaf fungus in high humidity.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 70–90 days — Harvest starts 2.5–3 months after planting
hidroponik.nutrisi: AB Mix EC 2.0–3.0, high potassium during fruiting
hidroponik.tips: Hydroponic bell peppers need stake support since the plant gets quite heavy when fruiting. Raise the nutrient EC from 2.0 to 2.5–3.0 once it enters the flowering and fruiting phase.

toxicity.note: Ripe fruit ok; foliage toxic, capsaicin irritates

name: Temulawak (Javanese Ginger)
description: A medicinal plant native to Indonesia with a large orange rhizome. It helps boost appetite, supports liver health, and strengthens immunity.

careDetails.soil: Loose, fertile soil rich in organic matter
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, keep the soil moist
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.tips: Temulawak isn't suited to hydroponics because the harvest is an underground rhizome that needs a solid medium to develop. It grows more effectively in a deep pot or in the garden.

toxicity.note: Curcuma rhizome non-toxic; safe in small amounts

name: Sambung Nyawa (Longevity Spinach)
description: A medicinal plant believed to help lower blood sugar, blood pressure, and cholesterol. The leaves can be eaten raw or juiced.

careDetails.soil: Loose, fertile soil
careDetails.watering: Water 2–3 times a week
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 30–45 days — Harvest young leaves gradually
hidroponik.tips: Longevity spinach grows very fast in hydroponics. Harvest young leaves from the top regularly to encourage new growth. Well suited to NFT systems or indoor racks.

toxicity.note: Edible leaf; limited pet toxicity data — use caution

name: Aloe Vera
description: A versatile succulent whose leaf gel is beneficial for burns, skin irritation, and digestive health. Very easy to care for.

careDetails.sunlight: Full sun or bright indirect light
careDetails.soil: Cactus/succulent mix with excellent drainage
careDetails.watering: Water infrequently, let the medium dry out completely
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.tips: Aloe vera isn't suited to hydroponics because, as a succulent, it needs dry periods between waterings. A constantly wet medium will cause root rot quickly.

toxicity.note: Aloe latex/saponins — vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy

name: Brotowali
description: A climbing medicinal plant with green, uniquely textured stems. It tastes bitter but is highly beneficial for diabetes, fever, and boosting immunity.

careDetails.soil: Fertile soil with good drainage
careDetails.watering: Water 2–3 times a week
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.tips: Brotowali is a woody climbing plant better grown in the ground or a large pot. It isn't efficient for a hydroponic system.

toxicity.note: Very bitter medicinal vine; limited pet safety data

name: Black Cumin
description: A herb with beautiful purple flowers and highly beneficial black seeds. Called 'habbatussauda' in Islamic tradition, it's known as an all-purpose remedy for various conditions.

careDetails.soil: Loose soil with good drainage, pH 6.0–7.0
careDetails.watering: Water moderately, avoid overwatering
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 90–120 days — Harvest the seeds once the pods have dried on the plant
hidroponik.tips: Black cumin can be grown hydroponically through the vegetative phase, but for optimal seed harvest it's better to move it to a soil medium. Keep the temperature from getting too hot (>30°C).

toxicity.note: Seeds edible in small amounts; excess may harm pets

name: Basil
description: A fresh aromatic herb that's a kitchen essential. Basil leaves lend a distinctive aroma to fresh vegetable platters, pasta, and Italian dishes. Fast-growing and productive.

careDetails.soil: Fertile medium, rich in organic matter, well-draining
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, keep the medium moist
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 25–35 days — Harvest leaves periodically to encourage growth
hidroponik.tips: Basil is the most productive hydroponic herb! Pinch off flower buds as soon as they appear so the plant focuses on leaf production. Harvest from the top regularly.

toxicity.note: Basil is non-toxic and safe for cats, dogs

name: Coriander / Cilantro
description: An important kitchen spice with a strong, distinctive aroma. Both the fresh leaves (cilantro) and the dried seeds are used in Indonesian, Indian, and Mexican cooking.

careDetails.sunlight: Full sun 5–6 hours, shade during the hot midday
careDetails.soil: Light medium with good drainage
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, keep the medium moist
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 30–45 days for leaves — Harvest young leaves periodically
hidroponik.tips: Coriander prefers cool temperatures. In hot areas, grow it indoors with supplemental lighting. Avoid transplanting the plant since it's sensitive to root disturbance.

toxicity.note: Coriander/cilantro is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Green Onion
description: The most frequently used kitchen seasoning in Indonesian cooking. It grows very fast and is easy to regrow — cut the base and replant for the next harvest.

careDetails.soil: Loose, fertile soil or potting medium
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, keep the medium consistently moist
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 20–30 days — Can be harvested in stages from the same plant
hidroponik.tips: The easiest method: place leftover green onion bases from cooking in a glass of water — they'll grow back in 3–5 days! For a larger harvest, use a simple NFT or DWC system.

toxicity.note: Allium — damages red blood cells; toxic to pets

name: Cinnamon
description: A tree that produces the fragrant cinnamon bark spice. It takes years before the bark can be harvested, but it can be pruned and enjoyed as an aromatic ornamental plant.

careDetails.soil: Fertile sandy soil, pH 4.5–6.0
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, keep the medium moist but not soggy
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.tips: Cinnamon is a hardwood tree that needs fertile soil and a strong root system. It isn't practical for hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Cinnamon mildly irritating; oils risky in quantity

name: Citronella Grass
description: An aromatic grass that produces citronella oil, used as a natural mosquito repellent. Unlike culinary lemongrass, its aroma is stronger and it isn't used as a seasoning.

careDetails.soil: Fertile soil with good drainage
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, tolerates mild drought
careDetails.fertilizer: Organic fertilizer or compost once a month is enough; avoid excess chemical fertilizer so the plant stays safe to consume.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and rhizome/root rot if drainage is poor.

hidroponik.tips: Citronella grass is a large grass better grown in the ground or a big pot outdoors. Its dense fibrous root system makes it inefficient for hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Cymbopogon essential oils may cause stomach upset

name: Oregano
description: An aromatic herb characteristic of Italian and Mediterranean cooking. Both dried and fresh leaves add a strong aroma to pizza, pasta, and soups. It grows compact and productive.

careDetails.soil: Light sandy medium with excellent drainage
careDetails.watering: Water when the medium is dry, tolerates drought
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply light fertilizer once a month; over-fertilizing actually reduces aroma and essential oil content.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and fungus due to poor air circulation.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 40–50 days — Harvest young leaves periodically
hidroponik.tips: Oregano is very productive in indoor hydroponics with a grow light. Make sure the EC isn't too high — oregano prefers moderate nutrients. Regular harvesting encourages new growth.

toxicity.note: Oregano essential oils — vomiting, diarrhea in pets

name: Thyme
description: A Mediterranean aromatic herb with small leaves and tiny purple flowers. Warm and distinctive in aroma, it pairs well with roast chicken, soups, and herbal tea. It also works as a ground cover.

careDetails.soil: Light sandy medium with excellent drainage
careDetails.watering: Water infrequently, let the medium dry out
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply light fertilizer once a month; over-fertilizing actually reduces aroma and essential oil content.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and fungus due to poor air circulation.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 45–60 days — Harvest fresh tips periodically
hidroponik.nutrisi: AB Mix EC 1.0–1.6, lighter than other herbs
hidroponik.tips: Thyme prefers to dry out between waterings — in hydroponics, make sure there's good aeration. A Kratky system with a sufficiently large air gap works well for thyme.

toxicity.note: Thyme mild; concentrated oils may upset stomach

name: Lemon Balm
description: A refreshing lemon-scented aromatic herb, a member of the mint family. Used for relaxing teas, room fragrance, and to help reduce stress and insomnia.

careDetails.soil: Fertile medium with good drainage
careDetails.watering: Water when the top of the medium is dry
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply light fertilizer once a month; over-fertilizing actually reduces aroma and essential oil content.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Aphids, mites, and fungus due to poor air circulation.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 30–45 days — Harvest young leaves for fresh tea
hidroponik.tips: Lemon balm grows very vigorously in hydroponics — it can become invasive! Prune regularly and harvest plenty to keep the plant compact. The aroma is strongest before flowering.

toxicity.note: Lemon balm is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Hydroponic Cherry Tomato
description: The most popular mini tomato variety for home gardening. It fruits abundantly year-round, has a refreshing sweet-tart flavor, and is highly productive in hydroponic systems.

careDetails.soil: Fertile, organic-rich medium or hydroponics
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, keep moisture consistent
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 60–75 days — First harvest 2–2.5 months after planting
hidroponik.nutrisi: AB Mix EC 2.0–3.5, high potassium during fruiting
hidroponik.tips: Cherry tomatoes are a hydroponic favorite — productive and fast. Set up string or stakes for support. Prune the suckers regularly. Pollinate the flowers by shaking the plant while it's in bloom.

toxicity.note: Ripe fruit safe; foliage and unripe fruit toxic

name: Melon
description: A premium sweet fruit popular as a table fruit. Grown hydroponically in a greenhouse, it produces high-quality melons with a sweeter, more consistent flavor.

careDetails.soil: Hydroponic medium or fertile, well-draining soil
careDetails.watering: Scheduled drip irrigation, avoid fluctuations
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.media: Rockwool slab / perlite + rice husk charcoal
hidroponik.waktu_panen: 70–90 days — Ripe fruit is marked by cracking at the stem
hidroponik.nutrisi: AB Mix EC 2.0 (vegetative) rising to 3.0–4.0 (fruit set)
hidroponik.tips: Hydroponic melons need tight management. Limit to 1–2 fruits per plant for premium size. Support the fruit with netting in the greenhouse. Reduce water and raise the EC a week before harvest to increase sugar content (Brix).

toxicity.note: Melon flesh is non-toxic and safe for pets

name: Blueberry
description: An antioxidant-rich blue berry known as a 'superfood'. It's challenging to grow in Indonesia because it needs cool temperatures, but it can succeed in the highlands.

careDetails.soil: Acidic soil pH 4.5–5.5, rich in peat/cocopeat
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, keep the medium moist
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 12–24 months to fruit for the first time after planting
hidroponik.nutrisi: Special low-pH blueberry AB Mix, EC 1.5–2.0
hidroponik.tips: Blueberries need a very low pH (4.5–5.5) that differs from other hydroponic plants. Use an acidic medium and monitor pH closely. In Indonesia, they're better suited to Malang, Dieng, or cool areas above 800 m elevation.

toxicity.note: Blueberry is non-toxic and safe for cats, dogs

name: Starfruit
description: A unique star-shaped tropical fruit with a refreshing sweet-tart flavor. Productive year-round and suitable for growing in a large pot. Popular as fresh fruit and in pickled preserves.

careDetails.soil: Fertile soil with good drainage, pH 5.5–6.5
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, avoid drought during fruiting
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.tips: Starfruit is a tropical fruit tree that needs deep soil and a strong root system. It isn't practical for hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Oxalates and caramboxin — risky, especially for dogs

name: Water Apple
description: A local Indonesian fruit that's crisp, juicy, and refreshing. The tree is shady and productive, making it suitable as both a shade tree and a fruit producer in the yard.

careDetails.soil: Fertile soil, tolerates high moisture
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, tolerates wet soil
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.tips: Water apple is a large tropical tree that needs to be planted in the ground or a large-volume pot (>100L). It isn't practical for a home hydroponic system.

toxicity.note: Water apple fruit generally safe for pets

name: Passion Fruit
description: A climbing tropical fruit with an exotic sweet-tart flavor and a distinctive aroma. Its flowers are very beautiful, making it a dual-purpose plant that's both ornamental and fruit-bearing.

careDetails.soil: Fertile soil with good drainage, pH 6.0–7.0
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, avoid waterlogging
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: 180–270 days — First fruiting 6–9 months after planting
hidroponik.nutrisi: AB Mix EC 1.8–2.5, increase potassium during fruiting
hidroponik.tips: Hydroponic passion fruit needs a trellis or arbor to climb. Start with a low EC (1.5), then raise it gradually. Hand-pollinate with a small brush to improve fruit set.

toxicity.note: Ripe pulp ok; unripe fruit, leaves release cyanide

name: Fig
description: The fig fruit mentioned in the Quran, with high historical and health value. It's becoming increasingly popular to grow in pots in Indonesia because it's adaptable and its fruit is unique.

careDetails.soil: Well-draining potting medium, add sand
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, let the medium dry out a bit between waterings
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.tips: The fig is a woody tree that needs a large pot or direct soil for optimal results. Its root system isn't suited to hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Ficus sap, ficin, psoralens — irritation, vomiting

name: Avocado
description: A superfood fruit rich in healthy fats, vitamins, and minerals. It's becoming increasingly popular as a potted plant in Indonesia. It takes 3–5 years to fruit from seed, sooner from grafting.

careDetails.soil: Fertile soil with good drainage, pH 6.0–7.0
careDetails.watering: Water regularly, avoid overwatering
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.tips: The avocado is a large tree that needs a deep, strong root system. It isn't suited to home hydroponics. Plant it in a large-volume pot (>50L) or directly in the ground.

toxicity.note: Persin in leaves, bark, pit — vomiting, diarrhea

name: Mini Date Palm
description: A palm tree that produces nutrient-rich dates. In Indonesia it can be grown as an exotic ornamental palm, though fruiting requires a dry climate.

careDetails.sunlight: Full sun, a maximum of 10+ hours/day
careDetails.soil: Sandy medium with perfect drainage
careDetails.watering: Water infrequently, very drought-tolerant
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilizer monthly; add a high-phosphorus and high-potassium fertilizer as flowering and fruiting approach.
careDetails.pruning: Periodically prune old, dry, or damaged leaves and stems to encourage new shoots and keep the plant tidy.
careDetails.commonProblems: Fruit flies, aphids, and fungal diseases on leaves or fruit.

hidroponik.tips: The date palm needs desert conditions to fruit optimally. In Indonesia it can grow but rarely fruits because the air humidity is too high.

toxicity.note: Date palm (Phoenix) non-toxic; fruit safe pitted

name: Fiddle Leaf Fig
description: A statement houseplant with large, glossy, violin-shaped leaves that brings a bold tropical look indoors.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 3-4 cm of soil is dry, roughly once a week; never let it sit in water.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light near a window; avoid harsh direct sun that scorches the leaves.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Prune the top to control height and remove damaged leaves to encourage branching.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown spots and leaf drop from overwatering, drafts, or sudden location changes.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new leaves appear every 4–6 weeks once established
hidroponik.tips: This fig hates root disturbance, so transition slowly and keep conditions stable; it sulks and drops leaves with any shock.

toxicity.note: Ficus sap irritates mouth and skin; causes vomiting in pets

name: Spider Plant
description: An easygoing, air-purifying plant with arching striped leaves and dangling baby plantlets.

careDetails.watering: Keep lightly moist; water every 4-5 days and let the top of the soil dry slightly between waterings.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates moderate shade but variegation fades in low light.
careDetails.fertilizer: Light liquid fertilizer once a month; over-feeding causes brown leaf tips.
careDetails.pruning: Trim brown tips and snip off plantlets to propagate or tidy the plant.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown leaf tips from fluoride in tap water or over-fertilizing.

hidroponik.metode: Kratky / DWC — also roots readily in plain water
hidroponik.media: Hydroton or net pot with no medium
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — plantlets form within 1–2 months
hidroponik.tips: One of the easiest plants for water culture; simply place a plantlet in water and it roots within a week or two.

toxicity.note: Safe for cats and dogs; mild stomach upset only if eaten in excess

name: Jade Plant
description: A hardy succulent with thick, glossy oval leaves, regarded as a symbol of good luck and prosperity.

careDetails.watering: Water sparingly, every 1-2 weeks, only once the soil is fully dry; it stores water in its leaves.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright light with several hours of direct sun keeps it compact and healthy.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted succulent fertilizer every 2 months during active growth.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back leggy stems to encourage a bushy, tree-like shape.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mushy stems and dropped leaves from overwatering; leaf shrivel if too dry.

hidroponik.tips: As a drought-loving succulent, the jade plant rots quickly in constant moisture and is not suited to hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Toxic to cats and dogs; causes vomiting, lethargy, incoordination

name: Boston Fern
description: A lush, feathery fern with gracefully arching fronds that thrives in humid, shaded spots.

careDetails.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist; water every 2-3 days and never let it dry out completely.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light or light shade; direct sun browns the fronds.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim brown or dead fronds at the base to keep the plant full.
careDetails.commonProblems: Crispy, browning fronds from low humidity or dry soil.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new fronds unfurl within 1–2 months
hidroponik.tips: Its love of constant moisture makes it suit semi-hydroponics well, but keep humidity high and refresh the solution every 2 weeks.

toxicity.note: Safe and non-toxic for both cats and dogs

name: Chinese Money Plant
description: A charming plant with round, coin-shaped leaves on slender stems, prized for its tidy, modern look.

careDetails.watering: Water about once a week when the top of the soil feels dry; avoid soggy roots.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; rotate it regularly so it grows evenly.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month in spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Remove yellowing lower leaves and pot up the baby offshoots it produces.
careDetails.commonProblems: Curling or yellow leaves from overwatering, drafts, or too little light.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — offshoots appear within 1–2 months
hidroponik.tips: Pilea transitions to LECA easily and its baby plantlets root quickly in plain water, making propagation simple.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic and safe for cats and dogs

name: String of Pearls
description: A trailing succulent with strands of round, pea-like beads that cascade beautifully from hanging pots.

careDetails.watering: Water every 1-2 weeks, letting the soil dry fully; the beads store water like a succulent.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light with a little morning sun; too little light makes strands sparse.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted succulent fertilizer every 2 months during growth.
careDetails.pruning: Trim leggy or bare strands and tuck cuttings back to fill out the pot.
careDetails.commonProblems: Shriveled or mushy beads from underwatering or overwatering respectively.

hidroponik.tips: As a drought-adapted succulent, its delicate beads rot quickly in standing water, so it is not suited to hydroponics.

toxicity.note: Toxic to cats and dogs; causes vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, lethargy

name: Hoya / Wax Plant
description: A trailing succulent vine with thick, waxy leaves and clusters of fragrant, star-shaped flowers.

careDetails.watering: Let the medium dry out between waterings, roughly every 7-10 days; reduce in winter.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; some morning sun encourages blooming.
careDetails.fertilizer: Apply diluted liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Trim leggy vines, but never cut the old flower spurs as they rebloom.
careDetails.commonProblems: Mealybugs hide under leaves; yellowing leaves signal overwatering.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (expanded clay) or rockwool for cuttings
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — rooted cuttings establish in 4-6 weeks
hidroponik.tips: Allow the medium to nearly dry between top-ups; Hoya roots rot easily if kept constantly wet.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs per ASPCA

name: English Ivy
description: A vigorous trailing or climbing vine with lobed evergreen leaves, ideal for hanging baskets.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium lightly moist; water when the top 2 cm feels dry, about every 5 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; variegated forms need brighter light to keep their pattern.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during spring and summer.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch back regularly to keep it bushy and control its spread.
careDetails.commonProblems: Spider mites thrive in dry air; brown crispy leaves mean too little humidity.

hidroponik.media: Hydroton (expanded clay) or plain water for cuttings
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — cuttings root within 2-3 weeks
hidroponik.tips: Ivy roots readily in water; refresh the solution weekly to prevent algae and rot.

toxicity.note: Triterpenoid saponins cause vomiting, drooling, diarrhea

name: Areca Palm
description: A graceful clumping palm with arching feathery fronds that brings a tropical feel indoors.

careDetails.watering: Keep the medium evenly moist but never soggy; water about every 3-4 days.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; avoid harsh direct sun that scorches the fronds.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer monthly during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Cut away only fully brown fronds at the base; avoid trimming green tips.
careDetails.commonProblems: Brown leaf tips from low humidity or fluoride in tap water; spider mites in dry air.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — new fronds emerge over 2-4 months
hidroponik.tips: Transition young plants by rinsing soil from roots; keep humidity high to ease the switch.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs per ASPCA

name: African Violet
description: A compact flowering houseplant with velvety leaves and clusters of purple, pink, or white blooms.

careDetails.watering: Water from below with room-temperature water when the surface is dry; keep leaves dry.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light; an east-facing window is ideal for steady blooming.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted bloom-boosting fertilizer every two weeks while flowering.
careDetails.pruning: Remove spent flowers and damaged outer leaves to encourage new blooms.
careDetails.commonProblems: Cold water leaves spots on leaves; crown rot from water pooling in the center.

hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — blooms appear within 2-3 months
hidroponik.tips: A wicking setup suits their need for steady moisture without wetting the crown or leaves.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs per ASPCA

name: Inch Plant / Wandering Jew
description: A fast-growing trailing plant with striking purple-and-silver striped leaves, perfect for hanging pots.

careDetails.watering: Water when the top 2 cm is dry, about every 3-4 days; it wilts quickly if too dry.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright indirect light keeps the purple coloring vivid; fades in low light.
careDetails.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer once a month during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Pinch the tips often to keep it full and prevent bare, leggy stems.
careDetails.commonProblems: Leggy growth in low light; lower leaves drop if overwatered or too shaded.

hidroponik.media: Plain water or hydroton (expanded clay) for cuttings
hidroponik.waktu_panen: Not harvested — cuttings root in just 1-2 weeks
hidroponik.tips: One of the easiest plants to root in water; refresh the solution weekly for vigorous roots.

toxicity.note: Sap may cause skin irritation and mild stomach upset

name: Echeveria
description: A charming rosette succulent with plump, powdery blue-green leaves that thrives on neglect.

careDetails.watering: Soak thoroughly only when the soil is completely dry, roughly every 1-2 weeks.
careDetails.sunlight: Bright direct light or full morning sun keeps the rosette compact and colorful.
careDetails.fertilizer: Diluted succulent fertilizer every two months during the growing season.
careDetails.pruning: Remove dried bottom leaves; behead and replant the rosette if it stretches.
careDetails.commonProblems: Etiolation (stretching) in low light; root rot and mushy leaves from overwatering.

hidroponik.tips: Echeveria is a dry-loving succulent whose roots rot in constant water, so it is not suited to hydroponic systems.

toxicity.note: Non-toxic to cats and dogs per ASPCA
